Mtn To Compel Arbitration, To Stay Action Pending Arbitration And Dismiss Paga Allegations; Table Of Non-Cal Authorities
Off Calendar Nov-16-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RORY C. QUINTANA OF KUMIN SOMMERS LLP, TEL. NO. 434-4500 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. STEVEN D. ALLISON OF THE DORSEY LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (714) 800-1402 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT U-HAUL COMPANY OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion To Compel Arbitration, To Stay Action Pending Arbitration And Dismiss Paga Allegations is Granted. Defendant established that the electronic signature is attributable to plaintiff per CC 1633.9(a) and thus the arbitration agreement is valid.
The PAGA waiver is unenforceable per Brown v. Ralph's Grocery Company (2011) 197 Cal.App.4th 489, 502-03. However, the PAGA waiver is severable from the rest of the agreement because the PAGA waiver is the only portion of the agreement that is unconscionable and severing the PAGA waiver would not require reformation of the entire agreement. The PAGA waiver is therefore severed from the rest of the agreement. Plaintiff is required to arbitrate all of his claims except the PAGA claims. The PAGA claims are stayed pending the outcome of the arbitration.
ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Court Reporter: Lavena Ward, CSR #7077 =302/HK
